ksa_text: Skill in conducting privacy impact assessments (PIAs) and data protection impact assessments (DPIAs) for new data processing activities and AI systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.

top_anchors.element_label: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA SP-S-001 (SP, Skill) does not clear the weak fit inclusion threshold of 0.52. Composite score 0.4804 is insufficient to establish relevance to clinical data management, regulatory compliance, and healthcare analytics at IC-to-senior-IC level. Framework signals (eu_ai_act=0.480, nist_ai_rmf=0.476, nice=0.399) do not demonstrate sufficient alignment with core analytics/BI work.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 exclude upheld. Score 0.4804 below threshold for weak fit domain; no rescuing analytics text signals. No internal inconsistencies identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does SP-domain KSA SP-S-001 (composite 0.4804) appear in published data analyst or BI role profiles?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: SP domain content (AI governance, security/privacy, risk management, compliance) is not a primary expectation in IC-to-senior-IC ANL roles per NIST CSF, ISO 27001; security competencies in IC ANL scope limited to data access controls awareness, not security architecture. Composite 0.4804 confirms weak signal. Exclusion consistent with published ANL frameworks.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 exclude upheld. External evidence confirms SP domain KSA at composite 0.4804 is not a standard requirement for Clinical Data Manager at IC-to-senior-IC level.

decision_rationale: Pipeline consensus: exclude. P2 exclude confirmed by P3 (internal) and P4 (external) adversarial agents. Zero overturns. Composite 0.4804, domain SP, scope weak fit. High confidence.
